# UC-22.50.26 · COBIT APO13.01 — ISMS establishment: security-policy compliance and control-effectiveness monitoring

## Description

Monitors ISMS control effectiveness by domain, calculating maturity levels aligned to COBIT APO13.01's requirement to establish and maintain an information security management system. Removes the erroneous per-group distinct count and adds ISMS maturity classification.

## Value

APO13.01 requires establishing and maintaining an ISMS aligned with enterprise strategy. Continuous compliance monitoring across control domains provides auditable evidence of ISMS effectiveness.

## Implementation

(1) Ingest GRC/policy-compliance scan results with control_id, control_domain, and status; (2) schedule weekly; (3) escalate domains below 100% effectiveness to CISO dashboard. Indexes required: `index=grc_compliance`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=control_assessment`, `sourcetype=policy_compliance`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Security policy audit logs, GRC platform exports, ES notable events, configuration-compliance scans.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest GRC/policy-compliance scan results with control_id, control_domain, and status; (2) schedule weekly; (3) escalate domains below 100% effectiveness to CISO dashboard.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc_compliance sourcetype IN (policy_compliance, control_assessment) earliest=-30d
| eval control_effective=if(status IN ("pass", "compliant", "effective"), 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_controls sum(control_effective) AS effective_controls values(eval(if(status NOT IN ("pass", "compliant", "effective"), control_id, null()))) AS failing_controls BY control_domain
| eval effectiveness_pct=round(100*effective_controls/total_controls, 1)
| eval isms_maturity=case(effectiveness_pct>=95, "Optimized", effectiveness_pct>=85, "Managed", effectiveness_pct>=70, "Defined", effectiveness_pct>=50, "Repeatable", true(), "Initial")
| where effectiveness_pct<100
| sort effectiveness_pct
| table control_domain, total_controls, effective_controls, effectiveness_pct, isms_maturity, failing_controls
```

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.

## SPL

```spl
index=grc_compliance sourcetype IN (policy_compliance, control_assessment) earliest=-30d
| eval control_effective=if(status IN ("pass", "compliant", "effective"), 1, 0)
| stats count AS total_controls sum(control_effective) AS effective_controls values(eval(if(status NOT IN ("pass", "compliant", "effective"), control_id, null()))) AS failing_controls BY control_domain
| eval effectiveness_pct=round(100*effective_controls/total_controls, 1)
| eval isms_maturity=case(effectiveness_pct>=95, "Optimized", effectiveness_pct>=85, "Managed", effectiveness_pct>=70, "Defined", effectiveness_pct>=50, "Repeatable", true(), "Initial")
| where effectiveness_pct<100
| sort effectiveness_pct
| table control_domain, total_controls, effective_controls, effectiveness_pct, isms_maturity, failing_controls
```

## Visualization

Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `control_domain`, `control_effective`, `domains_assessed`, `effective_controls`, `effectiveness_pct`, `failing_controls`.
